response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior generalist (TypeScript/Node.js, DLang) with genuine distributed-systems exposure, but the role's core requirement is 8 years of C++ and this resume shows only a ~6-month C++/C stint at National Instruments plus a student thesis project. C++ is effectively a hard-miss against an 8-year minimum, and there is no production cloud-compute-platform or formal technical-leadership-of-infra-engineers track record. The distributed-systems and tooling background is real but not aligned enough to offset the central language and platform gap.
strengths: Strong distributed-systems experience (micro-services handling millions of req/sec at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby)
Broad senior-level full-stack and infra/DevOps exposure incl. Kubernetes, Docker, CI/CD, AWS CDK
Clear mentoring/teaching inclination via workshops, dojos, and multi-module courses
concerns: C++ experience is ~6 months plus a thesis project, far below the 8-year minimum
No production cloud-compute-platform architecture or large-scale infra build in C++
No evidence of a formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers

key_factors.factor: Lack of formal infra tech-lead/mentoring role
